architecture_fit.note: Recurrent frontier @ adjacency genuinely propagates reachability h hops; loose 'attention' framing (no softmax/QK) but a valid minimal hand-coded circuit that solves the task.

baseline_comparison.note: Sweep contrasts model F1=1.0 against the 1-hop strawman degrading 1.0→0.34 as hops grow; lift_over_linear_baseline=0.659 at the horizon.

faithfulness.note: Mechanism is the model (hand-built), so the causal story is direct, but no explicit ablation/patching and README only gestures at first_pass collapsing to one-hop.

operating_range.note: Holds perfectly across the full hop axis (1–5), the goal's relevant axis; demo varies p/seed but no quantitative sweep over graph density or N.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Entirely hand-set with zero learned parameters — Q=identity frontier, K=V=adjacency — exactly reproducing BFS.

visual_judgement.note: Per-hop propagation heatmap shows the frontier expanding like a wave, with GT-overlay prediction and a model-vs-baseline sweep plot; slightly busy 4-panel but legible.

visualisation_rationale.note: README ties the step-by-step view and GT overlay to the multi-hop claim and the sweep to beating the 1-hop baseline; well-justified.

notes: A clean hand-coded recurrent-attention circuit that implements exact multi-hop BFS, scoring perfect F1=1.0 across the entire hop sweep with full robustness and a strong 0.66 lift over the degrading 1-hop baseline. The hardcoded, zero-parameter construction earns the bonus tier, and the per-hop propagation visualisation makes the mechanism legible. Held back from top marks by a loose 'attention' framing (no real QK/softmax) and the absence of an explicit ablation or density/scale sweep beyond the hop axis.
